turns.topic: Revise fetch-items.sh and run in background

Added JSONL_FILE=items-all-goethe-faust.json
Added JSONL append on successful fetch
Added SKIP backfill: if file exists but not in JSONL, append it
Added counters: fetched / skipped / failed
Tested with limit=10 — all OK
Ran full script in background (task bbd8438)

turns.user: The script execution was interrupted, so far 34,248 items have been fetched. Can you confirm or revise fetch-items.sh such that you can continue fetching the remaining items?
turns.assistant: Identified O(n²) grep bottleneck in SKIP logic. Replaced with sorted temp file pre-loaded at startup using a single Python pass. Fixed bash 3 compatibility (no declare -A). Tested and confirmed resumption works correctly.
actions: Identified grep-per-SKIP performance issue
Replaced with sorted temp file (JSONL_IDS_TMP) pre-loaded at startup via Python
Used grep -Fxq against sorted file for O(log n) lookup
Added trap to clean up temp file on EXIT
Fixed bash 3 compatibility (macOS ships bash 3.2, no associative arrays)
Tested with limit=15 — 15 SKIPs confirmed
Resumed full script in background (task bc51e7a)
